topic: Marie signed them up for a cooking class

turns.content: Marie signed us up for a cooking class. Italian. six weeks. Saturday mornings. I did not volunteer for this.
Ha — sounds like you were informed rather than consulted. What kind of cooking class is it — hands-on at a kitchen studio, or more of a demonstration thing?
hands on. some place in Center City. they give you a station and you cook the dish together. first class is homemade pasta. from scratch.

Making pasta from scratch is actually a pretty satisfying skill to pick up. Flour, eggs, a little work with your hands — it's straightforward once you get the feel for the dough. Have you done much cooking beyond the basics?
I can work a grill. I can make eggs. at the firehouse I do a decent chili. but pasta from scratch is not something I've ever thought about doing. I buy it in a box like a normal person.

Fair enough — boxed pasta gets the job done. But there's a real difference in texture when it's fresh. And honestly, if you can handle a grill and make a good chili, you've got the fundamentals. The pasta class might surprise you. What made Marie want to sign up?
she said we need to do something together that doesn't involve sitting on the couch. her words. she's not wrong but I wasn't going to tell her that.

That's a fair point from her side, and smart of you not to concede it out loud. Cooking classes are actually one of the better couples activities — you're doing something with your hands, there's food at the end, and it's not like ballroom dancing. Could be worse.
listen if she had said ballroom dancing I would have filed for divorce. I'm kidding. but barely. cooking I can live with. at least there's a meal at the end of it.

Exactly — worst case scenario, you eat well for six Saturdays. And you might actually pick up a few things. Italian cooking is mostly about good ingredients and not overcomplicating it, which sounds like it fits your general approach to life.

that's the thing. I don't need it fancy. just make it right. use good stuff. don't mess it up by doing too much. I tell the guys at work the same thing about fireground operations. keep it simple. execute.
Same principle applies in the kitchen. If the first class goes well, you might actually enjoy it. And Marie gets her Saturday mornings out of the house. Sounds like a decent deal all around.

we'll see. I told her I'd give it an honest shot. if I come out of this making a decent red sauce I'll call it a win. she's already talking about week three being risotto. I don't even know what risotto is.
Risotto is just rice cooked slowly with broth and stirred a lot — it turns out creamy and rich. Takes patience but it's not complicated. You might be a natural at it.
patience I got. you learn that working a 24. alright. I'll give it a shot. worst case I eat Italian food for six Saturdays and Marie's happy. not the worst deal.
That's the spirit. And if you end up actually enjoying it, you don't have to admit it out loud. Give the first class an honest shot — you might surprise both of you.
